2005 CAR Training

January • February 2005
Journalism & NewsroomInvestigative MethodsTechnology

IRE and NICAR offer specialized boot camps and training seminars to teach journalists how to acquire electronic information, analyze data using spreadsheets and databases, and develop impactful stories, with follow-up support provided after training.

These unique seminars, taught by IRE and NICAR's experts, train journalists to acquire electronic information, use spreadsheets and databases to analyze the information and to translate that information into high-impact stories. In addition, IRE and NICAR provide follow-up help when participants return to their news organizations.

Boot Camps: Columbia, Mo. • March 20-25 • May 15-20 • Aug. 7-12

Mapping Data for News Stories Boot Camps: Columbia, Mo. Uncover news stories by mapping data with geographic information system software. • Aug. 19-21

Boot Camps for Editors: Columbia, Mo. Tailored to the needs of newsroom managers. • April 8-10

Advanced CAR Statistics: Tempe, Ariz. (ASU) Aimed at strengthening the skills of reporters who want to move beyond basic CAR. • Feb. 18-20

For more information: www.ire.org/training/bootcamps.html
